heading: Nuclear test monitoring equipment: furnishing to foreign governments
label: 2565 Nuclear test monitoring equipment: furnishing to foreign governments

children.heading: .—Authority To Transfer Title to or Otherwise Provide Nuclear Test Monitoring Equipment
children.label: a .—Authority To Transfer Title to or Otherwise Provide Nuclear Test Monitoring Equipment

children.text: transfer title or otherwise provide to a foreign government (A) equipment for the monitoring of nuclear test explosions, and (B) associated equipment;
as part of any such conveyance or provision of equipment, install such equipment on foreign territory or in international waters; and
inspect, test, maintain, repair, or replace any such equipment.
to provide the United States with timely access to the data produced, collected, or generated by the equipment; and

to permit the Secretary of Defense to take such measures as the Secretary considers necessary to inspect, test, maintain, repair, or replace that equipment, including access for purposes of such measures.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: c .—Report
children.text: Promptly after entering into any agreement under subsection (b), the Secretary of Defense shall submit to Congress a report on the agreement. The report shall identify the country with which the agreement was made, the anticipated costs to the United States to be incurred under the agreement, and the national interest of the United States that is furthered by the agreement.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Delegation
children.label: d .—Limitation on Delegation
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may delegate the authority of the Secretary to carry out this section only to the Secretary of the Air Force. Such a delegation may be redelegated.
